sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. شفر to scratch, to scrape (cognate to צָפַר, סָפַר); hence to polish.

(2) to be bright, prop. to be polished (compare Arab. سفر I. IV., to shine forth as the dawn, and שְׁפַרְפָּרָא), to be beautiful, i.q. Ch. and Syr. Followed by עַל to please any one, Psa 16:6, compare Dan 4:24. The notion of being bright is also applied to brilliancy of sound (compare, on the other hand, הָלַל); whence שֹׁופָר trumpet.

(3) i.q. Ethiop. safara, to measure; whence אֶשְׁפָּר a measure; which see. (Cogn. is סָפַר No. 3, to number.) As to the passage, Job 26:13, see שִׁפְרָה .
